# terrible_ngine_io_cloudstack_host (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) Name of the host.
- `zone` (String) Name of the zone in which the host should be deployed.

### Optional

- `allocation_state` (String) Allocation state of the host.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `cluster` (String) Name of the cluster.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `host_tags` (String) Tags of the host.
- `hypervisor` (String) Name of the cluster. Required if *state=present* and host does not yet exist. Possible values are `KVM`, `VMware`, `BareMetal`, `XenServer`, `LXC`, `HyperV`, `UCS`, `OVM`, `Simulator`.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `password` (String) Password for the host. Required if *state=present* and host does not yet exist.
- `pod` (String) Name of the pod. Required if *state=present* and host does not yet exist.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) State of the host.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `url` (String) Url of the host used to create a host. If not provided, `http://` and param *name* is used as url. Only considered if *state=present* and host does not yet exist.
- `username` (String) Username for the host. Required if *state=present* and host does not yet exist.

### Read-Only

- `capabilities` (String) Capabilities of the host.
- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `cluster_type` (String) Type of the cluster of the host.
- `cpu_allocated` (String) Amount in percent of the host's CPU currently allocated.
- `cpu_number` (String) Number of CPUs of the host.
- `cpu_sockets` (Number) Number of CPU sockets of the host.
- `cpu_speed` (Number) CPU speed in Mhz
- `cpu_used` (String) Amount of the host's CPU currently used.
- `cpu_with_overprovisioning` (String) Amount of the host's CPU after applying the cpu.overprovisioning.factor.
- `created` (String) Date when the host was created.
- `disconnected` (String) Date when the host was disconnected.
- `disk_size_allocated` (Number) Host's currently allocated disk size.
- `disk_size_total` (Number) Total disk size of the host
- `events` (String) Events available for the host
- `gpu_group` (String) GPU cards present in the host.
- `ha_host` (Boolean) Whether the host is a HA host.
- `has_enough_capacity` (Boolean) Whether the host has enough CPU and RAM capacity to migrate a VM to it.
- `host_type` (String) Type of the host.
- `host_version` (String) Version of the host.
- `hypervisor_version` (String) Hypervisor version.
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `ip_address` (String) IP address of the host
- `is_local_storage_active` (Boolean) Whether the local storage is available or not.
- `last_pinged` (String) Date and time the host was last pinged.
- `management_server_id` (Number) Management server ID of the host.
- `memory_allocated` (Number) Amount of the host's memory currently allocated.
- `memory_total` (Number) Total of memory of the host.
- `memory_used` (Number) Amount of the host's memory currently used.
- `network_kbs_read` (Number) Incoming network traffic on the host.
- `network_kbs_write` (Number) Outgoing network traffic on the host.
- `os_category` (String) OS category name of the host.
- `out_of_band_management` (String) Host out-of-band management information.
- `removed` (String) Date and time the host was removed.
- `resource_state` (String) Resource state of the host.
- `suitable_for_migration` (String) Whether this host is suitable (has enough capacity and satisfies all conditions like hosttags, max guests VM limit, etc) to migrate a VM to it or not.
